rbaHistory.note: First cut since Nov 2020 — CPI back in 2-3% band
Held; trimmed mean CPI above target
Held; Q1 CPI surprised to upside

news.title: ASX edges lower, BHP extends gains; Coles shares fall after court loss
news.snippet: The Australian sharemarket was little changed in early trade, as miners advanced, but banks continued their slide and the nation’s supermarket giants declined.

news.title: ‘Bunch of crap’: Mark Bouris slams budget changes to CGT and negative gearing
news.snippet: Finance guru Mark Bouris is “pissed off” after Tuesday’s federal budget and claims Labor’s excuses for axing property investor tax breaks are a “bunch of crap”.

news.title: Labor’s spending plan in ‘face of adversity’
news.snippet: The Albanese government’s fifth federal budget has delivered a controversial backflip on a key tax, but Jim Chalmers is defending it as necessary.

news.title: Bad signs for ASX after CBA carnage
news.snippet: After investors dumped Commonwealth Bank shares on Wednesday, the Australian sharemarket is pointing towards losses again on Thursday.

news.title: Big Hits ��� Haranga Resources, NexGen Energy and Greentech Metals
news.snippet: Bulls N’ Bears Big Hits looks at notable drill intercepts reported to the ASX last week, led by Haranga Resources’ gold hits, NexGen Energy’s uranium and Greentech Metals’ platinum.

news.title: ‘They’re worried’: Telling gap in forecasts
news.snippet: Two sets of numbers, same crisis, vastly different outcomes. What the Reserve Bank knows that Treasury doesn’t want you to see.

news.title: Supply shock: The Iran war will change the oil market forever
news.snippet: The latest International Energy Agency assessment of the oil market points to a continuing shortfall of oil supply, and higher prices, even if the war were to end next month.

news.title: ‘Monumental call’: One policy change Labor may regret forever
news.snippet: Treasurer Jim Chalmers now owns Aussie house prices. And whatever happens next, he is the man that will have to answer for it.

news.title: ‘Labor cannot be trusted’: Pauline Hanson takes aim at budget broken promises
news.snippet: Pauline Hanson has blasted Labor over broken promises in the budget, saying the major tax changes show the government “can’t be trusted”.

news.title: ‘Misleading’: Coles loses Down Down fight
news.snippet: Supermarket giant Coles has lost a major court case with a judge finding it engaged in “misleading” marketing through its Down Down campaign.

news.title: Perth biotech Syngenis unleashes AI drug discovery push
news.snippet: Perth biotech Syngenis is using AI to slash the time and cost of discovering RNA therapies, targeting a booming global market that is reshaping modern medicine.

news.title: Flagship tables monster 2.1M ounce gold resource in Chile
news.snippet: Flagship Minerals has tabled a monster 2.1-million-ounce gold resource at its Isidora project in Chile’s prolific Maricunga Gold Belt.
